message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="orange" summary="CSS review: no blocking, 4 minor consistency fixes, approved">
## CSS/Frontend Review: Step 5 — Clarify as Next Action + Trash

Overall this is a clean, compact implementation. The inbox item template was refactored well to incorporate the clarify controls inline. Here is my categorized feedback:

---

### BLOCKING

None. The implementation is solid from a CSS/frontend perspective.

---

### Minor

**1. `.inbox-item__clarify-button:hover` uses `opacity: 0.9` instead of `background-color` change.**

Line 1246: `opacity: 0.9` is inconsistent with every other button hover in the codebase, which all use `background-color: var(--color-primary-dark)`. Opacity-based hover is subtler and less predictable across backgrounds. This should follow the established pattern:

```css
.inbox-item__clarify-button:hover {
    background-color: var(--color-primary-dark);
}
```

**2. `.inbox-item__clarify-button` uses `color: var(--color-bg)` instead of `white`.**

Line 1236: Every other primary button in the codebase uses `color: white` for text on `--color-primary` backgrounds (base layer buttons, `.landing-hero__cta`, `.next-action-item__edit-save`, `.context-edit-form__save`, `.todo-item__delete-yes`). Using `var(--color-bg)` is technically the same value visually (since `--color-bg` maps to `--color-gray-50` which is near-white), but it is semantically wrong -- `--color-bg` is a page background token, not a "text on primary" token. If we ever introduce dark mode where `--color-bg` becomes dark, this button text would become invisible.

Change to `color: white` for consistency and correctness.

**3. `.inbox-item__context-select` has redundant styles from the base layer.**

Lines 1225-1232: Same pattern as the Step 4 minor issue. The `border`, `border-radius`, `color`, and `background` are already handled by the base layer's `select` rule. The component only needs layout-specific overrides:

```css
.inbox-item__context-select {
    font-size: var(--font-size-sm);
    padding: var(--space-xs);
    inline-size: auto;
}
```

Note: `inline-size: auto` is needed to override the base layer's `inline-size: 100%` on selects, since this select sits in a flex row.

**4. `.inbox-item__context-select` uses `background: var(--color-bg)` instead of `var(--color-bg-raised)`.**

Line 1230: The base layer uses `background-color: var(--color-bg-raised)` (white) for form inputs. Using `var(--color-bg)` (gray-50) is inconsistent. On the page background (also gray-50), the select will have no visual distinction from the surrounding area. Should be `var(--color-bg-raised)` to match other inputs.

---

### Nice-to-have

**5. The `flex-wrap: wrap` on `.inbox-item` (line 1190) is a good addition.**

On narrow viewports, this allows the actions row to wrap below the title. Smart mobile-first thinking -- the previous inbox item did not have this.

**6. No transition on the clarify button.**

The clarify button has no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` transition rule. Other primary buttons in the base layer get transitions automatically, but since the component overrides `background` explicitly, the base transition may not apply cleanly. Adding a transition is optional but would feel more polished:

```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) {
    .inbox-item__clarify-button {
        transition: background-color var(--transition-fast) ease;
    }
}
```

---

### Summary

No blocking issues. Four **Minor** items, all consistency fixes: opacity hover, wrong color token for button text, redundant select styles, and wrong background token on the select. These are quick fixes -- each is a single property change. The HTML structure is semantically correct, the template properly passes contexts, and the clarify/trash flow works cleanly with progressive enhancement.

**CSS/Frontend: Approved** (minor items are non-blocking).

</teammate-message>

content.text:
Acknowledged -- Lea's CSS review for Step 5 noted. These are minor consistency items that are non-blocking. For Step 7, I'll make sure Carson is aware of the established CSS patterns (especially the correct hover and color token conventions) when he builds the clarify-as-project form.

The team is still processing stale review messages. I remain focused on Step 7 and ready to review Carson's work as soon as it arrives.
